texto: Most of my friends like words too well. They set them under the blinding light of the poem and try to extract every possible connotation from each of them, every temporary pun, every direct or indirect connection - as if a word could become an object by mere addition of consequences. Others pick up words from the streets, from their bars, from their offices and display them proudly in their poems as if they were shouting, "See what I have collected from the American language. Look at my butterflies, my stamps, my old shoes!" What does one do with all this crap?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jack Spicer: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Most of my friends like words too well. They set them under the blinding light of the poem and tr…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, letters-to-lorca, poetry pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e termino.

texto: Words are what sticks to the real. We use them to push the real, to drag the real into the poem. They are what we hold on with, nothing else. They are as valuable in themselves as rope with nothing to be tied to.
explicacao: Citação de Jack Spicer sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Words are what sticks to the real. We use them to push the real, to drag the real into the poem. …” articula reflexao, letters-to-lorca, poetry em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como poetry e words.

frase_original: Words are what sticks to the real. We use them to push the real, to drag the real into the poem. They are what we hold on with, nothing else. They are as valuable in themselves as rope with nothing to be tied to.

texto: And I think that it is certainly possible that the objective universe can be affected by the poet. I mean, you recall Orpheus made the trees and the stones dance and so forth, and this is something which is in almost all primitive cultures. I think it has some definite basis to it. I'm not sure what. It's like telekinesis, which I know very well on a pinball machine is perfectly possible.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jack Spicer: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “And I think that it is certainly possible that the objective universe can be affected by the poet…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, poetry, telekinesis pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de telekinesis.
